title: English muffins
subtitle: Yeasted griddle-baked muffins with nooks and crannies
excerpt: English muffins are baked on a griddle, not in an oven. The characteristic nooks-and-crannies interior that catches butter comes from tearing the muffin apart with a fork rather than cutting it with a knife.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136 — 'muffins' in the bread chapter are the yeasted griddle-baked variety, not the American cup-baked kind. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): the fork-splitting method and the cornmeal dusting are identified as standard British practice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Fork-split, wrap individually, and freeze. Toast from frozen.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cook the day before and store in a bag at room temperature. Toast before serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: The method of opening an English muffin by inserting the tines of a fork around the circumference and prying the two halves apart rather than cutting with a knife. This preserves the irregular interior texture — the nooks and crannies that give the muffin its character and catch the butter.

content.text: English muffins are cooked on a griddle, not in the oven. The dry heat from below sets the base while the top steams slightly inside the ring, producing a soft, pale crown. The interior is achieved by
the cooked muffin when it's ready to serve — running a fork around the equator and pulling the halves apart. A knife compresses the crumb and loses those pockets.
Mix, knead, and bulk ferment, 1 hour

Combine {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{yeast-fast-action}}, {{salt-table}}, and {{caster-sugar}}. Add the {{whole-milk}}, {{water}}, and {{unsalted-butter}}. Mix to a soft, slightly sticky dough and knead for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic. The dough will feel softer than a typical bread dough — that extra hydration is what creates the open interior. Cover and leave until doubled, about 1 hour.

Dust the work surface generously with {{semolina}}. Roll the dough to about 2 cm thickness and cut into 9–10 cm rounds. Place on a semolina-dusted tray, dust the tops, cover, and prove for 20 minutes until slightly puffed.

Heat a flat griddle or heavy frying pan over the lowest heat setting. Cook the muffins without oil for 7–8 minutes per side until deep gold with a pale, slightly matte surface on top. The base should be firmly set when you press it gently. Work in batches; keep finished muffins warm in a low oven. Fork-split to serve.
